chunk.text: Panos Kotseras Senior Consultant – Nickel, Stainless Steel and Special Alloys CRU Metal Pages NiCoMo 2013 London, 12-13 March 2013 The Medium-Term Outlook for Nickel and Cobalt 2 Structure • Nickel contemporary trends & prices • Nickel production • Nickel consumption • Nickel market balance & price forecast • Conclusions • Cobalt contemporary trends & prices • Cobalt production • Cobalt consumption • Cobalt market balance & price forecast • Conclusions Part 1 Part 2 3 Structure • Nickel contemporary trends & prices • Nickel production • Nickel consumption • Nickel market balance & price forecast • Conclusions • Cobalt contemporary trends & prices • Cobalt production • Cobalt consumption • Cobalt market balance & price forecast • Conclusions Part 1 Part 2 Contemporary trends in the nickel market 4 • Prices under pressure due to an oversupplied market • Consumption supported by Chinese stainless sector • Asia contributed to nickel mine production growth • Chinese nickel pig iron production higher in 2012 • Market surplus expected to continue into 2013

relations.evidence: Contemporary trends in the nickel market: Prices under pressure due to an oversupplied market

chunk.text: Conclusions 15 • Prices currently under pressure, macro causes volatility • In the medium-term, new tech to support production rises • Chinese NPI output grew strongly; expected to continue • Consumption supported by expanding Chinese stainless • Macro-level risks remain; so do demand downside risks • Production delays may generate bigger market deficits 16 Structure • Nickel contemporary trends & prices • Nickel production • Nickel consumption • Nickel market balance & price forecast • Conclusions • Cobalt contemporary trends & prices • Cobalt production • Cobalt consumption • Cobalt market balance & price forecast • Conclusions Part 1 Part 2 Contemporary trends in the cobalt market 17 • Low market prices • Low price volatility • Industry consolidation • Structural change in raw materials • Increasing dependence on batteries Source: Ryan’s Notes Cobalt prices less volatile and in decline due to oversupply Historical transaction prices, $/lb 18 0 5 10 15 20 25 01-2011 06-2011 12-2011 06-2012 12-2012 High grade (99.8%) Low grade (99.3%)

chunk.text: Conclusions 30 • Weak price environment challenges participants • Industry consolidation is taking place • Importance of copper operations in the DRC • Decline in raw material inventories in China • Strong demand but rising importance of one end use • Oversupply to impact prices in the medium term • LME cobalt contract saw growth in 2012 For enquiries on cobalt: Panos Kotseras Senior Consultant CRU panayiotis.kotseras@crugroup.com Thank you for your attention 31 The Medium-Term Outlook for Nickel and Cobalt For enquiries on nickel: Nikhil Shah Principal Consultant CRU nikhil.shah@crugroup.com
